operational_knowledge_units.title: System bag out closes destinations and exchanges totes containing parcels
operational_knowledge_units.summary: The slide states that a system bag out closes all destinations and exchanges all totes that contain parcels.
observed_evidence: Bullet: 'System Bag out will close all destinations and exchange all totes with parcels in them.'
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Overall System Bag Out Control: system bag out closes all destinations and exchanges all totes with parcels in them.

operational_knowledge_units.title: Bag out can be performed by sorter leg
operational_knowledge_units.summary: The slide indicates that a user may choose to perform bag out by leg of the sorter rather than system-wide.
observed_evidence: Bullet: 'A user could also choose to bag out by leg of the sorter.'
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Bag Out Control supports bag out by leg of the sorter as an alternative scope to full system bag out.

operational_knowledge_units.title: Cancel bag out cancels exchange tasks not already in process during manual bag out
operational_knowledge_units.summary: For a manual bag out event, Fortna offers a cancel bag out function that cancels any exchange task that is not already in process.
observed_evidence: Bullet: 'In the event of a manual bag out Fortna offers a cancel bag out that will cancel any exchange task that is not in process.'
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: During manual bag out, cancel bag out cancels exchange tasks that are not yet in process.

operational_knowledge_units.title: Pre bag out exchanges the lowest 25 percent of destinations per sorter leg
operational_knowledge_units.summary: The slide defines a pre bag out behavior that exchanges the lowest 25% of destinations for each leg of the sorter.
observed_evidence: Bullet: 'Pre bag out exchanges the lowest 25% of destinations per leg of the sorter.'
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Pre bag out behavior: exchange the lowest 25 percent of destinations per leg of the sorter.

procedure_candidate_attempts.title: Initiate bag out by sorter leg
preconditions: Bag out capability is available in the system
An operator or user needs to perform bag out for a specific sorter leg
procedure_candidate_attempts.procedure_visual_summary: Slide titled 'Overall System / Bag Out Control' with bullets describing system bag out, bag out by leg, cancel bag out, and pre bag out behavior.

steps.instruction: Choose to bag out by leg of the sorter.
steps.expected_outcome: Bag out is scoped to a sorter leg instead of the entire system.
steps.validation_check: Confirm the intended bag out scope is by sorter leg.
steps.visual_region_hint: No specific UI region shown; evidence comes from slide bullet text.

warnings: The slide does not show the UI path, control name, or full execution sequence for this action.
procedure_candidate_attempts.retrieval_text: Procedure candidate: bag out by leg of the sorter. Slide only explicitly states that a user can choose this scope; detailed UI steps are not provided.

procedure_candidate_attempts.title: Cancel manual bag out exchange tasks not in process
preconditions: A manual bag out event is in progress or has been initiated
There are exchange tasks associated with the bag out
procedure_candidate_attempts.procedure_visual_summary: Slide titled 'Overall System / Bag Out Control' describing cancel bag out behavior during manual bag out.

steps.instruction: Use cancel bag out during a manual bag out event.
steps.expected_outcome: Exchange tasks that are not already in process are canceled.
steps.validation_check: Verify that only exchange tasks not in process are canceled.
steps.visual_region_hint: No specific UI region shown; evidence comes from slide bullet text.

warnings: The slide does not provide the exact trigger, UI location, or command sequence for cancel bag out.
The slide explicitly limits cancellation to exchange tasks that are not in process.
